commentaries.author: Arnobius of Sicca
commentaries.content: That they have reached the light they knew not of, thanks to lewdness? For we, lest any one should chance to think that we are ignorant of, do not know, what befits the majesty of that name, assuredly

commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: If he called them gods, unto whom the word of God came, and the scripture cannot be broken; Cornelius a Lapide If He called them gods unto whom the word of God came, whom the Word of God appointed judges and gave them authority by Moses and his successors, and commanded them to judge rightly as partaking His authority, making them (says Euthymius) gods, as it were, upon earth. And the Scripture cannot be broken: no one, i. e, can take from them the name of Judges , which the irrevocable word of Scripture has given them.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
